name: dashkit
description: Widget dashboard builder with drag-and-drop resizable grid, plugin widget system, and ActionPanel for adding widgets. For configurable user dashboards with DnD layout.
when_to_use: Widget dashboard builder with drag-and-drop resizable grid, plugin widget system, and ActionPanel for adding widgets
avoid: Not for static dashboard layouts — use CSS grid/flex. Not for simple page layouts — the plugin registration overhead is only justified for user-configurable widget grids.

name: ActionPanel
description: Renders a horizontal toolbar of action items that can optionally be dragged onto the dashboard canvas. Supports CSS transition animations on show/hide and integrates with the DnD context for drag-to-add behaviour.
when_to_use: When you need a palette of widget types that users can drag onto the dashboard grid.
When you want a toolbar of contextual actions shown during dashboard edit mode.
When you want animated show/hide of an action bar controlled by a boolean flag.
avoid: Do not use for per-widget overlay controls — use overlayControls prop on DashKit instead.
Do not use outside a DashKitDnDWrapper when dragProps are configured, otherwise drag events will not propagate.

props.name: items
props.description: Array of action items to display in the panel.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class applied to the root element.

props.name: disable
props.description: When true the panel is hidden (or animated out when toggleAnimation is enabled).

props.name: toggleAnimation
props.description: Enables CSS transition animation when the panel mounts and unmounts.

name: DashKit
description: The root dashboard component that renders a responsive, draggable and resizable grid of plugin widgets. Exposes static helpers to register plugins, manage configuration, and reflowlayout, plus instance methods to reload widgets and retrieve item metadata.
when_to_use: When building a configurable dashboard where users can rearrange, resize, add and remove widgets.
When you need a plugin-based widget system where each widget type is registered independently.
When you require autoupdate polling for widgets at a configurable interval.
avoid: Do not use when a simple static layout suffices — the plugin registration and Config overhead is not needed for read-only pages.
Do not nest multiple DashKit instances without wrapping them in a shared DashKitDnDWrapper when cross-group drag-and-drop is needed.

props.name: config
props.description: Dashboard configuration object containing items, layout, aliases and connections.

props.name: editMode
props.description: Enables drag, resize and overlay controls when true.

props.name: onChange
props.type: (data: { config: Config; itemsStateAndParams: ItemsStateAndParams; groups?: DashKitGroup[] }) => void
props.description: Called when the layout or item state changes.

props.name: context
props.description: Arbitrary context object passed down to every plugin widget.

props.name: defaultGlobalParams
props.description: Default parameter values applied to all widgets.

props.name: draggableHandleClassName
props.description: CSS class that acts as the drag handle; if omitted the whole item is draggable.

props.name: focusable
props.description: Makes grid items focusable via keyboard and fires onItemFocus/onItemBlur.

props.name: globalParams
props.description: Global parameter overrides merged into each widget's params.

props.name: groups
props.description: Optional group definitions that split the grid into independent layout sections.

props.name: itemsStateAndParams
props.description: External state and params map keyed by item id.

props.name: noOverlay
props.description: Disables the edit-mode overlay and controls on all items.

props.name: onCopyFulfill
props.description: Called after a widget copy action completes.

props.name: onDragStart
props.description: Called when a drag operation starts on any grid item.

props.name: onDrag
props.description: Called during an active drag operation.

props.name: onDragStop
props.description: Called when a drag operation ends.

props.name: onDrop
props.description: Called when an external item is dropped onto the grid.

props.name: onItemBlur
props.description: Called when a focusable grid item loses focus.

props.name: onItemEdit
props.description: Called when the user clicks the settings (edit) overlay control.

props.name: onItemFocus
props.description: Called when a focusable grid item receives focus.

props.name: onItemMountChange
props.type: (item: ConfigItem, state: { isAsync: boolean; isMounted: boolean }) => void
props.description: Called when a widget mounts or unmounts.

props.name: onItemRender
props.description: Called after a widget finishes rendering (including async load).

props.name: onResizeStart
props.description: Called when a resize operation starts.

props.name: onResize
props.description: Called during an active resize operation.

props.name: onResizeStop
props.description: Called when a resize operation ends.

props.name: overlayControls
props.description: Custom overlay control buttons keyed by plugin type. Pass null to disable all overlay controls.

props.name: overlayMenuItems
props.description: Items shown in the overlay dropdown menu. Pass null to disable the menu.

props.name: getPreparedCopyItemOptions
props.description: Transforms copy options before they are written to localStorage.

props.name: settings
props.description: Runtime settings including autoupdateInterval (seconds) and silentLoading flag.

name: DashKitDnDWrapper
description: Context provider that enables drag-and-drop from external sources (e.g. ActionPanel) onto the DashKit grid. Manages shared drag state and exposes callbacks for drag lifecycle events.
when_to_use: When you want to drag items from an ActionPanel or external source onto the DashKit grid.
When multiple DashKit instances need to share a single drag context for cross-grid drops.
When you need to intercept drop events to preview or constrain where items can be dropped.
avoid: Do not use when you only need internal grid reordering — DashKit handles that natively without this wrapper.
Do not wrap components unrelated to the DashKit grid as the context is specific to dashkit drag mechanics.

props.name: children
props.description: A single React element that will have access to the DnD context (typically wraps both ActionPanel and DashKit).

props.name: dragImageSrc
props.description: URL of the image shown under the cursor while dragging. Defaults to a transparent 1x1 pixel.

props.name: onDragEnd
props.description: Called when the drag operation ends.

props.name: onDragStart
props.description: Called when a drag operation starts, receiving the dragged item's props.

props.name: onDropDragOver
props.type: (draggedItem: DraggedOverItem, sharedItem: DraggedOverItem | null) => void | boolean
props.description: Called while a dragged item is over a drop zone; return false to prevent the drop.

examples:
console.log('dragging', props)}>
;

name: PluginText
description: Built-in DashKit plugin that renders plain or markdown-transformed text inside a dashboard widget. When an apiHandler is provided it fetches rendered HTML asynchronously and shows a loader during the request.
when_to_use: When you need a simple text or markdown widget on the dashboard.
When you want server-side markdown rendering integrated with DashKit's async load lifecycle.
avoid: Do not use for complex interactive widgets — create a custom plugin instead.
Do not pass raw user HTML directly; use a safe markdown-to-HTML transform in apiHandler.

props.name: data
props.description: Widget data containing the text string to render.

props.name: apiHandler
props.description: Async function that converts raw text to HTML. When omitted the text is rendered as plain text.

name: PluginTitle
description: Built-in DashKit plugin that renders a heading widget with configurable size. Supports standard size tokens ('xl' | 'l' | 'm' | 's' | 'xs') or custom fontSize/lineHeight font params, and can be anchored in a table of contents.
when_to_use: When you need a section heading or label widget on the dashboard.
When you want the heading to be linkable from a table of contents (showInTOC).
avoid: Do not use for rich text content — use PluginText with markdown support instead.

props.name: data
props.type: { size: PluginTitleSize | TitleFontParams; text: string; showInTOC: boolean } & PluginWidgetProps['data']
props.description: Widget data with the text, size and table-of-contents visibility flag.

name: useDnDItemProps
description: Returns HTML drag event props for an ActionPanelItem when a DashKitDnDWrapper context is present. Returns null when no DnD context is available or the item has no dragProps.
when_to_use: When building a custom ActionPanel item container that needs native HTML5 drag behaviour connected to DashKit's DnD context.
avoid: Do not call outside a DashKitDnDWrapper — the hook will return null and dragging will be inert.
signature: useDnDItemProps(item: ActionPanelItem): { draggable: true; unselectable: 'on'; onDragStart: DragEventHandler; onDragEnd: DragEventHandler } | null

parameters.name: item
parameters.description: The action panel item whose dragProps will be used to configure the drag event handlers.
